channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Eliminated Morocco turn attention to cohosting 2030 World Cup
item.description: Morocco were eliminated from the 2026 World Cup by a 2-0 defeat by France in the quarterfinals, but host next edition.

item.title: Clinical Sinner demolishes Djokovic to set up Wimbledon final with Zverev
item.description: Sinner is targeting his fifth Grand Slam title, which would bring him within two of the tally of rival Carlos Alcaraz.

item.title: Indian cricket board to review T20 team’s ‘bad phase’ after England defeats
item.description: India slumped to a nine-wicket defeat in the fourth T20 against England, following a 2-0 series whitewash in Ireland.

item.title: UK police arrest man suspected of murdering former MP Ann Widdecombe
item.description: Reform UK politician Widdecombe, 78, was found dead at her home on Thursday with serious injuries.

item.title: Peru’s President-elect Fujimori says she wants to restore ties with Mexico
item.description: Keiko Fujimori has 'every intention' of ending months-long rift with Mexico over its political asylum decisions.

item.title: Hospital workers wounded in Israeli drone attack on Gaza’s Kamal Adwan
item.description: Palestinian Health Ministry denounces attack as part of Israel's 'systematic targeting of health facilities'.

item.title: Ronaldo still has Portugal future if he wants it, says new coach Jesus
item.description: The 2026 World Cup exit for Portugal was believed to be the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's international career.

item.title: FIFA has nearly 1,200 tickets still on sale for World Cup final at $7,380
item.description: Several tickets remain for lower decks ranging from $19,995 to $32,970, as well as Trophy Lounge section at $34,500.

item.title: Russian attacks kill four as Ukraine continues to target oil infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian official says Russia's attack on Kramatorsk kills four, including a teenager, and injures nine.

item.title: Hundreds give hero’s welcome to Egypt after best World Cup run
item.description: Supporters waved Egyptian and Palestinian flags in support of Gaza as the Pharaohs boarded an open-top bus for a parade.

item.title: Trump hints at further Iran negotiations after exchange of fire over Hormuz
item.description: Iran has not responded to Trump's latest claim, which comes as new attacks threaten the full resumption of war.

item.title: Who is Fadel Shaker? Lebanese pop star released on bail
item.description: In 2012, singing star Shaker quit his music career, before being accused of joining an armed group in Lebanon.

item.title: Ruthless Zverev spoils Fery’s Wimbledon dream to power into final
item.description: Alexander Zverev will face reigning Wimbledon champion Jannik Sinner or seven-time winner Novak ​Djokovic in the final.

item.title: Israel bans Jerusalem’s grand mufti from Al-Aqsa Mosque for one week
item.description: The incident is the latest in a pattern of Israeli measures in the occupied territory since the Gaza genocide began.

item.title: Spain vs Belgium LIVE: FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinal
item.description: Live coverage and text updates as Lamine Yamal's Spain face Belgium for the right to meet France in the semifinals.

item.title: Trump refuses to sign US housing bill over voting act standoff
item.description: The housing legislation will become US law at midnight with or without President Donald Trump's signature.

item.title: Family mourns baby after Israeli forces blocked journey to hospital
item.description: Three-month-old Ahmad Zaid’s family received his birth certificate and death certificate on the same day.

item.title: The search for Gaza’s missing brings heartbreak and closure for loved ones
item.description: Thousands of bodies remain trapped under the rubble of their homes in Gaza, with retrieval a long and difficult task.

item.title: Ukraine chokes fuel to Crimea, Russian consumers, targeting military supply
item.description: Ukraine’s unmanned systems have cut overland routes and begun to target Russian shadow tankers.

item.title: Israeli drones strike Lebanon despite US-brokered framework deal
item.description: The latest strikes reportedly injure two, as Amnesty urges probe into earlier Israeli attacks which killed 24 civilians.

item.title: Super typhoon as wide as France heads to East Asia
item.description: As Super Typhoon Bavi approaches, residents in Taiwan, Japan and China have been stockpiling supplies.

item.title: Ukrainian court detains alleged killers of Monaco bomb attack suspect
item.description: Motives for the killing of Anastasiia Berezovska remain unclear as the unfolding saga grips both countries.

item.title: Death, silence, and survival inside New Jersey State Prison
item.description: A firsthand account shows how isolation and neglect can make incarceration a slow form of death in U.S. prisons.

item.title: Andy Burnham apology for Labour stance on Gaza: Is UK’s position shifting?
item.description: Likely incoming UK PM hints at policy shifts towards Israel's genocidal war on Gaza. But analysts are wary.

item.title: Police probe donations to Britain’s far-right Reform party
item.description: Reform UK's finances draw police scrutiny as Farage fights to reclaim his seat in a by-election.
